summary: This case presents classic signs of vitamin C deficiency or scurvy including cutaneous manifestations like point hemorrhages and mucosal bleedings as well as hematological involvement such as haemorrhage into synovial spaces indicated by x-rays suggesting possible underlying nutritional insufficiency leading primarily from poor dietary habits characterized herein through 'junk' foods consumption patterns. To address this issue effectively requires immediate correction via supplementation alongside improved overall intake quality focusing particularly upon adequate levels of essential nutrients
